questions: Should the track settings popover (fit/scale/opacity/blend mode) remain accessible from the inline label, or should it move to a different UI location (e.g., a right-click context menu or inspector panel)?
Should the +Video/+Audio buttons appear as a footer row inside the canvas, or as floating buttons elsewhere in the timeline UI?

The current TIMELINE_START_LEFT is 20px. Moving labels inline increases this to ~144px, which reduces visible clip area. Is this acceptable, or should the inline label be narrower (e.g., icon + abbreviated text at ~80px)?
success_criteria: Track labels and clip rows are rendered inside the same scroll container — no separate scrolling column exists
Vertical scrolling keeps labels perfectly aligned with their rows at all times (no sync drift)
Horizontal scrolling keeps labels sticky at the left edge while clips scroll normally
Drag-to-reorder moves the entire row (label + clips) as a single visual unit
All existing clip interactions work: select, multi-select, drag between tracks, resize, split, delete, marquee select
External drag-drop from asset panel still places clips on correct tracks
Track label hover UI (rename, settings, delete) still functions
No regressions in coordinate calculations (clientXToTime, drop target resolution, clip positioning)
assumptions: The track settings popover will remain on the inline label (hover to reveal), matching current UX
Label width stays at 144px (w-36) to avoid redesigning the label UI; TIMELINE_START_LEFT increases accordingly
The +Video/+Audio buttons will be rendered as a footer inside the canvas below the last row
The existing dnd-kit sortable approach will be reused, just relocated to wrap entire rows inside the canvas
The TimeRuler component stays above the scrollable area (not inside it) — it only scrolls horizontally, not vertically, and this behavior is preserved
Labels use CSS `position: sticky; left: 0` for horizontal scroll anchoring — this is well-supported and works inside overflow containers
